  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.0,
    "R1.2": 1.0,

    "R1.6": 0.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 0.7,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.2", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": false,
  "clarification_question": "",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The user explicitly asks to check the logical consistency, making verification of the internal argument chain the load-bearing responsibility.",
    "RX.1": "Any assessment of consistency should disclose uncertainty about interpretations of the text or borderline contradictions.",
    "RX.2": "The review must avoid overclaiming by limiting conclusions to internal logic rather than broader empirical validity or design quality.",
    "RX.3": "Scope must remain confined to logical consistency and not drift into evidence alignment, novelty, or writing quality.",
    "RX.4": "Findings should be framed to avoid undue reputational harm or definitive judgments beyond what the logic check supports.",
    "RX.5": "Any identified inconsistencies should be tied to specific passages to ensure traceability and reproducibility of the assessment."
  }
}

rationale.R1.2: The user explicitly asks to check the logical consistency, making verification of the internal argument chain the load-bearing responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Any assessment of consistency should disclose uncertainty about interpretations of the text or borderline contradictions.
rationale.RX.2: The review must avoid overclaiming by limiting conclusions to internal logic rather than broader empirical validity or design quality.
rationale.RX.3: Scope must remain confined to logical consistency and not drift into evidence alignment, novelty, or writing quality.
rationale.RX.4: Findings should be framed to avoid undue reputational harm or definitive judgments beyond what the logic check supports.
rationale.RX.5: Any identified inconsistencies should be tied to specific passages to ensure traceability and reproducibility of the assessment.
